questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
State which of the following inequalities can be combined and which not? Write the combined form, if possible.
i) A > B, B > C
ii) B < A, B ≥ C
iii) A ≥ B, B < C
iv) A ≥ B, B ≤ C
v) A > B, C > D

solutions.solution: By first golden rule, no combination is possible in case of (ix) and (x) because they have four terms each. [(ix) has A, B, C and D while (x) has A, B, M and N]. Thus, they do not have a common term.
